title: CI probe output
description: Slice of repo-context produced by the CIProbe (Layer A, Phase 1; S4-01). Strict at the slice (`additionalProperties: false`) at every nested block, in keeping with Phase 1 ADR-0004. The slice is OPTIONAL at the envelope (ADR-0010): repos with no CI markers may emit a slice with provider=null but the envelope's required[] does NOT list `ci`. Warning IDs match the ADR-0007 pattern (`^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$`); per-file workflow parse failures emit the bare `ci.workflow_parse_error` ID and the offending path is recorded under `raw/ci.json`, not in the WarningId itself (per S4-01 validator CN-1 resolution).

provider.description: First-match provider in catalog precedence order. Null when no CI markers are detected.

additional_providers.description: Runner-up providers in catalog precedence order (NOT alpha-sorted). Empty for single-provider repos.

workflow_files.description: Lex-sorted list of parsed workflow file paths (relative to repo root). Files that fail to parse are OMITTED here and surfaced via `ci.workflow_parse_error` in `warnings`; the offending path is recorded under raw/ci.json.

builds_image.description: True when any parsed workflow contains a marker from _IMAGE_BUILD_MARKERS (docker build / docker buildx as run:, docker/build-push-action as uses:).

image_build_command.description: First image-build marker matched (verbatim run: or uses: text). Null when builds_image is false.

unit_test_command.description: First substring match against canonical test scripts (npm test, vitest, jest, pytest, go test, etc.) found in workflow run/script entries. Null when no marker matches.

smoke_test_command.description: First substring match against smoke-test scripts (npm run smoke, scripts/smoke.*) found in workflow run/script entries. Null when no marker matches.

references_secrets.description: Lexicographically sorted, deduplicated literal identifier names captured by the bounded `${{ secrets.NAME }}` regex across all parsed workflows. Values are NEVER resolved (production ADR-0005).

warnings.description: Soft-degrade signals. Each item matches the ADR-0007 ID pattern.

errors.description: Forward-compatible per-slice error IDs. Phase 1 routes parse failures to `warnings` (not here); this array stays empty.
